findings.title: SQL injection vulnerability
findings.description: User input is directly interpolated into SQL query without sanitization
findings.fix: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements

findings.title: Hardcoded API key
findings.description: API key is hardcoded in source code
findings.fix: Move API key to environment variables or secure configuration

findings.title: Hardcoded secret token
findings.description: Secret token is hardcoded in source code
findings.fix: Move secret to environment variables or secure vault

findings.title: Hardcoded database password
findings.description: Database password is hardcoded in source code
findings.fix: Use environment variables or secure configuration management

input.code:
import os
def run_command(user_input):
    os.system(f"ls {user_input}")
    return "Command executed"

findings.title: OS command injection
findings.description: User input is directly passed to os.system without sanitization
findings.fix: Use subprocess with shell=False and validate/sanitize input

findings.title: Cross-site scripting vulnerability
findings.description: User input is rendered in HTML without escaping
findings.fix: HTML escape user input or use safe template rendering

findings.title: Path traversal vulnerability
findings.description: User-controlled filename allows directory traversal attacks
findings.fix: Validate and sanitize filename, use os.path.join with safety checks

findings.title: Weak cryptographic hash
findings.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password hashing
findings.fix: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 for password hashing

findings.title: Insecure deserialization
findings.description: pickle.loads on untrusted data allows arbitrary code execution
findings.fix: Use safe serialization formats like JSON or validate data source

findings.title: Server-side request forgery
findings.description: User-controlled URL in server-side request without validation

findings.title: Open redirect vulnerability
findings.fix: Validate redirect URLs against whitelist of allowed domains

findings.title: Code injection vulnerability
findings.description: eval() on user input allows arbitrary code execution
findings.fix: Use ast.literal_eval for safe evaluation or implement proper sandboxing

{message}

findings.title: Hardcoded password
findings.description: Password is hardcoded in source code
findings.fix: Move password to environment variables or secure configuration

findings.title: SQL injection vulnerability
findings.description: User input is directly interpolated into SQL query

findings.title: OS command injection
findings.description: User input passed to shell command without sanitization
findings.fix: Use subprocess with proper input validation

findings.title: Cross-site scripting
findings.description: User input rendered in HTML without escaping

input.code:
import yaml
import hashlib
def process_config(config_data):
    config = yaml.load(config_data)
    hash_val = hashlib.sha1(config['password'].encode()).hexdigest()
    return hash_val

findings.title: Insecure YAML deserialization
findings.description: yaml.load() on untrusted data allows arbitrary code execution

findings.title: Weak cryptographic hash
findings.description: SHA1 is cryptographically weak for password hashing
findings.fix: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 for password hashing

findings.title: Commented hardcoded credential
findings.description: Password found in comments may indicate security risk
